column8: David's mum Frieda was useless when it came to fixing computer problems. It was in part because she didn't know how programs operated or how to____________the settings, but it was also because she didn't want to know. It simply wasn't of interest to her.
column9: So, whenever something went wrong, it was David to the rescue. He'd always ask what she had done to it, but the response was always the same; "I have no idea. It's just stopped working." David would have to______________upon a mission to work out what was the matter.
column10: It rarely took David long to solve the problem. He was a computer_____________who could take a computer apart and put it back together again. But he was going off to university soon, and what would his mum do then?
column11: "I suppose I'll just call someone", she said when David brought____________the topic in conversation.
column12: His mum managed a plan nursery and didn't need computers to run her shop, although it was nice for recording the details of her business______________ .
column13: She knew the basics of computers, which satisfied her needs. She had________________accustomed to placing orders online, but when the computer froze up, so did Davids' mum.
column14: She would just pull out her old-fashioned address book and call suppliers directly. As long as she could rely an a method that was tried and_____________, computer problems were of little concern.
